channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Key Updates for Employers on Work Injury Compensation Under Coida
item.description: The recent amendment to the Compensation for Occupational Injuries and Diseases Act (Coida) brings significant changes for employers in South Africa. Domestic workers, part-time and seasonal employees...

item.title: Strengthening Systems and Infrastructure is Key to Boosting African Trade
item.description: For years, Africa has focused heavily on trade agreements and policies, including the African Continental Free Trade Area, which is the continent's biggest integration effort yet. But as discussions a...

item.title: How the Catholic Church Determines the Dates of Holy Week and Easter Sunday
item.description: The article from GMA Integrated News explains how the dates of Easter Sunday and Holy Week are determined each year. According to Fr. Earl Gabriel Tan of the Diocese of Imus, Easter Sunday is celebrat...

item.title: Deputy President Paul Mashatile faces Parliament questions in scheduled Q&A session
item.description: Deputy President Paul Mashatile is appearing before the National Assembly in a scheduled question‑and‑answer session on Thursday, 19 March 2026. The live session allows Members of Parliament to pose w...

item.title: Asim Munir urges unity as Pakistan pauses anti-terror operations for Eid
item.description: Chief of Defence Forces and Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir reaffirmed Pakistan’s strong resolve to eradicate terrorism from all fronts, stressing that terrorists and their networks will be target...

item.title: Challenges Faced by Children with Intellectual Disabilities in Public Spaces in India
item.description: Children with intellectual disabilities in India often face significant difficulties in accessing public spaces and services due to both infrastructural shortcomings and social attitudes. Instances su...

item.title: Trump References Pearl Harbor During Talks With Japan Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: During a tense White House meeting, U.S. President Donald Trump met with Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi to discuss Japan's role in the ongoing Iran conflict. Trump praised Japan's support but ...

item.title: FIFA fines Israel for discrimination, rejects Palestinian complaint on West Bank clubs, and confirms Iran’s World Cup fixtures
item.description: FIFA has imposed a US$190,000 fine on the Israel Football Association for breaches of anti-discrimination rules, citing tolerance of politicized and militaristic messaging, racist behaviour by Beitar ...

item.title: Comelec extends registration deadline for political parties and sectoral organizations in BARMM elections
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has officially extended the period for political parties and sectoral organizations to file applications for accreditation in the upcoming Bangsamoro Autonomous R...

item.title: Most County Doctors Still Await Salary Adjustments Despite Overpayment Claims
item.description: The Kenya Medical Practitioners, Pharmacists and Dentists Union (KMPDU) has confirmed that majority of doctors working under county governments are still owed salary adjustments, despite government cl...

item.title: Federal government appoints Ayaz Shaukat as first head of Pakistan’s Social Media Regulatory Authority
item.description: The federal government of Pakistan has appointed Islamabad’s Advocate General, Ayaz Shaukat, as the inaugural chairman of the newly created Social Media Protection and Regulatory Authority (SMPRA). Th...

item.title: Community Efforts Restore Calm Along Turkana-West Pokot Border
item.description: Peace has returned to the volatile border between Turkana and West Pokot counties after months of bandit attacks and cattle rustling that left many dead and displaced families. Local leaders, security...

item.title: The Centenary of Winnie-the-Pooh: How Literature Supports Mental Wellbeing
item.description: This year marks the centenary of A.A. Milne's iconic children's book, Winnie-the-Pooh, illustrated by E.H. Shepard. The story of a bear in the Hundred Acre Wood, along with Christopher Robin and frien...

item.title: Profiles of Iran’s Senior Leadership Amid Recent U.S. Bounty Announcements
item.description: On March 15, 2026, the Trump administration announced a $10 million reward for information on several senior Iranian figures. Two of these leaders have already been killed in Israeli strikes, highligh...

item.title: US F-35 Makes Emergency Landing After Suspected Iranian Attack
item.description: Dem dey report say one US F-35 stealth fighter don get hit by suspected Iranian fire for Middle East and e don land emergency for American air base. US Central Command talk say di pilot dey okay and s...

item.title: CSK’s Nathan Ellis ruled out of IPL 2026 due to hamstring injury
item.description: Chennai Super Kings (CSK) have suffered a significant setback ahead of the IPL 2026 season, with Australian fast bowler Nathan Ellis being ruled out due to a hamstring injury. The 31-year-old, who was...

item.title: US Defence Secretary Confirms Open-Ended Military Campaign in Iran
item.description: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth stated on Thursday that there is no set timeframe for ending the ongoing US military campaign against Iran, which began three weeks ago in coordination with Israeli f...

item.title: Cyclone Narelle downgraded but still poses severe threat to far north Queensland
item.description: Cyclone Narelle, once classified as a Category 5 system, has been downgraded to Category 4 but remains an extremely dangerous storm bearing down on far north Queensland. The Bureau of Meteorology (BoM...

item.title: Rivers State Para Athletes Appeal To Governor Fubara For Funding To Attend Nigeria Para Games
item.description: Para athletes from Rivers State have appealed to Governor Sir Siminialayi Fubara to approve and release funds so they can participate in the third edition of the Nigeria Para Games in Abuja, holding f...

item.title: PTI Leaders Denied Access to Imran Khan Amid Health Concerns
item.description: On Thursday, leaders of Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) were once again refused permission to meet their party founder, Imran Khan, who remains imprisoned in Adiala Jail. Advocate Niazullah Niazi, spea...
